data.Challenge: The spacecraft is more reactive to changing thermal environments.
There is less real estate to be allocated to solar cells, designated radiator area, and/or viewports required for science instruments.
There is less space for electronic components, science instruments, and thermal control hardware. Components can be more thermally coupled, and it can be harder to isolate different thermal zones.
There is less power available for powered thermal control technology.
There is a big challenge to dissipate power as electronics are stacked close to each other, sometimes with no direct path to radiator.
MLI can “short” along the edges resulting in degraded performance, not specific to SmallSats but more of a general spacecraft issue.
